story.title: Dr. Osei and the Forest's Internet
story.text:
My name is Dr. Jabari Osei. I am a scientist in the rainforest of Panama. I study the amazing world under our feet.

Trees look separate above ground. But underground, they are connected. They use a network of fungi to share food. I call it the forest’s internet. My job is to understand how this system works.

Every day, I measure things in the soil. I check the amount of leaf litter. This is dead leaves that feed the fungi. I also measure the soil moisture. Fungi need water to grow their tiny threads. When there is more food and water, the mycorrhizal fungal network activity goes up. The network gets busy and strong. This leads to more nutrient transfer between trees. The trees can share more resources with each other.

I use a computer to model this system. My model helps me make predictions. I also think about other questions. Does the age of a network change how well it works? What happens if there are many different types of fungi? My research helps us protect these important forest ecosystems.

margin_defs.definition: A forest with tall trees and a lot of rain.
A simple way to show how something works.

check_prompts: What two things does Dr. Osei measure in the soil?
What happens to nutrient transfer when fungal network activity goes up?

vocabulary.definition: About a special fungus that works with plant roots.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The mycorrhizal network helps the trees get food.

vocabulary.definition: A partnership where two living things help each other.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The fungus and the tree have a mutualism relationship.

vocabulary.definition: The tiny, thin threads that make up a fungus.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The hyphae spread through the soil to connect trees.

vocabulary.definition: Food that plants and animals need to live and grow.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The soil has a lot of nutrient for the plants.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Osei mentioned network age. How might an older, 100-year-old network be different from a new, 10-year-old network?
discussion_cards.frame: I think an older network might be ___ because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: If a forest has very dry soil for a long time, what do you predict will happen to the nutrient transfer between trees? Why?
discussion_cards.frame: If the soil is dry, I predict that nutrient transfer will ___ because the fungal network needs ___.

boxes.label: Cause
boxes.content: More Leaf Litter (food) AND More Soil Moisture (water)

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
